citations.description: Ticket comment: '[operator-attested] AC2 is inherently post-deploy... The live canary-run verification (capture ticket id + rebar verify-authorship = verified) is the bounded post-deploy operator step; no code work remains.' — explicitly defers the live verification step and provides no captured ticket ID or verify-authorship output.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] A canary run (post-deploy; triggered via `workflow_dispatch` on `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`) creates a bug/comment/transition whose event is signed as Rebar Bot: capture the created ticket id from the run log and confirm `rebar verify-authorship` (or `rebar verify-identity`) reports it `verified` and attributed to identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`.
findings.detail: The ticket comment explicitly defers the live canary-run verification: it states 'The live canary-run verification (capture ticket id + rebar verify-authorship = verified) is the bounded post-deploy operator step; no code work remains.' No captured ticket ID from an actual post-deploy workflow_dispatch run is recorded, and no concrete `rebar verify-authorship` output confirming `verified` and identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d` is attached. The attestation references the code setup (Gerrit change 679) and the signing mechanism (local unit test), but explicitly acknowledges the required live run has not been performed. The operator-attested criterion requires ≥1 verifiable specific (a ticket id from the run log + verify-authorship output = verified), neither of which is present in the attestation.
findings.remediation: After the change lands on main, trigger reconcile-bridge-canary.yml via workflow_dispatch; capture the ticket ID printed in the run log; run `rebar verify-authorship <ticket-id>` (or `rebar verify-identity <ticket-id>`) and confirm it reports `verified` attributed to identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`. Record the GHA run URL, the captured ticket ID, and the full verify-authorship output as a ticket comment.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
